$comment: Phase 1 regulatory map for Kazakhstan (run-kz-2026-07-14). Evidence-linked; UNKNOWN where not captured.

laws.name: Law No. 193-VII 'On Digital Assets in the Republic of Kazakhstan'
laws.note: Foundational digital-asset law; Art. 11(5) prohibits organising circulation without a licence.

laws.name: Amendments 'On amendments and additions to certain legislative acts'
laws.note: Comprehensive framework: DFAs incl. stablecoins; NBK licensing of providers outside the AIFC.

laws.name: Presidential decree on digital-asset adoption
laws.note: Income-tax exemption for trades on licensed platforms; stablecoin settlement; tokenized instruments. Tier C source only.

regulators.name: National Bank of Kazakhstan (NBK)
regulators.scope: Licenses/registers unsecured digital-asset exchange operators and trading/DFA platform operators OUTSIDE the AIFC (since 2026-05-01); maintains registries.

regulators.name: Astana Financial Services Authority (AFSA)
regulators.scope: Regulator of the AIFC; licenses digital-asset activities in/from the AIFC; issues public warnings.

regulators.name: Agency for Regulation and Development of the Financial Market (ARDFM)
Financial Monitoring Agency (AFM)
regulators.scope: AML/CFT enforcement: platform closures, asset seizures.

regulators.name: Ministry of Artificial Intelligence and Digital Development

licensing_relevance_for_cbw.summary: Two lawful paths to serve Kazakhstan: AFSA licence (AIFC) or NBK licence/registration (since 2026-05-01). Of the six CBW exchanges only Bybit (Bybit Limited, AFSA-A-LA-2024-0027, Active) holds a verified local authorisation. OKX, Bitget and MEXC are explicitly named unlicensed by AFSA (2026-04-29). KuCoin and BingX: no authorisation found, not named in the warning.
